_meta.purpose: Zoning/building lookup database for the Native Suns Homes chatbot. Given a customer's Florida location, determine whether an ADU / manufactured / modular home qualifies there and under what conditions.
_meta.coverage: State of Florida (all 67 counties). County records are the baseline; municipalities override their county when the customer is inside city limits.
_meta.disclaimer: Zoning is set locally and changes often. Answers are guidance only, not a permit guarantee. Always confirm with the local building/zoning department before purchase.
_meta.coverage_status: All 67 counties have records. 40 are verified against county code/official pages (covering ~97% of Florida's population, including every metro and mid-size county). The remaining 27 are smaller rural counties on a shared 'rural baseline' (verified:false) where a county-specific ADU ordinance could not be confirmed online — each still carries the correct state-law framing and county-seat contact. The chatbot should present verified:false records with an explicit 'please confirm with the county' caveat. Note: several verified counties currently allow only non-rentable 'guest houses'/family units (no kitchen or no renting) rather than true ADUs — e.g. Charlotte, Highlands, Flagler, Brevard, Sumter — pending their Dec 1, 2026 state-mandated ADU ordinances. CITY RECORDS: 13 major cities now have municipality records nested under their county (Tampa, Orlando, St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Miami, Hialeah, Cape Coral, Port St. Lucie, Fort Lauderdale, Palm Bay, Gainesville, Kissimmee, Lakeland). Jacksonville is covered by the Duval record and Tallahassee by the Leon record (consolidated/city-led jurisdictions). City rules OVERRIDE the county record when the address is inside city limits. Cities marked verified:false (Clearwater, Miami, Hialeah) need size-cap confirmation. For any other city not listed, the chatbot must tell the customer to check with their city. MANUFACTURED DISTRICTS: per-district placement rules confirmed for Marion, Volusia, Pasco, Lake, Highlands, Charlotte, Lee, Polk, St. Johns (see manufactured_districts field); all other counties answer 'district-dependent' until researched - and from Jan 1, 2027 the HB 399 by-right rule applies statewide (see manufactured_home_law_2027).
_meta.state_law_update: Florida HB 943 — passed as CS/CS/SB 1730 (May 2025), amending F.S. 163.31771 — requires local governments to allow ADUs in any area zoned single-family residential and bars them from unreasonably increasing ADU construction cost or effectively prohibiting ADUs. Follow-on legislation (SB 48 / HB 313, 2026) directs every county and municipality to adopt a conforming ADU ordinance by Dec 1, 2026. KEY FOR THIS BUSINESS: the 2025 law amends the ADU definition to explicitly INCLUDE a manufactured home built on or after Jan 1, 2025 that meets HUD (National Manufactured Housing Construction and Safety) standards — so new manufactured homes qualify as ADUs under state law, which may preempt stricter local bans over time. Short-term (under 30 day) rentals of an ADU are prohibited. Practical effect: statewide the default answer is 'ADU likely allowed on a single-family lot, subject to local size/lot rules' — direct the customer to confirm specifics with the local zoning dept.

manufactured_home_wind_zones.note: HUD manufactured homes must be rated for the wind zone where they are installed — this gates WHICH units can be delivered WHERE, independent of zoning. A Wind Zone II unit cannot be installed in a Wind Zone III county.
manufactured_home_wind_zones.exposure_D: Homes installed within 1,500 ft of the coastline fall under Exposure D — must meet Wind Zone III standards PLUS reinforced anchoring/upgraded components (FAC 15C-1.01031).
manufactured_home_wind_zones.hvhz_note: Miami-Dade and Broward are additionally in the Florida Building Code High-Velocity Hurricane Zone — strictest standards for site-built/modular construction too.

universal_checks.note: Zoning is necessary but NOT sufficient. The chatbot should walk every customer through these gates regardless of county.
checks: City vs unincorporated: if the address is inside city limits, the CITY's rules override the county record — always ask first.
HOA / deed restrictions: an HOA can prohibit ADUs or manufactured homes even where zoning allows them. Always ask if there's an HOA.
FEMA flood zone: flood zones (especially V/coastal A) add elevation and foundation requirements and can change feasibility/cost.
Wind zone (manufactured only): see manufactured_home_wind_zones — unit rating must match the county.
Septic/well vs sewer: adding a dwelling usually requires proof of septic capacity or a sewer connection; FL Dept. of Health approval on septic parcels.
Impact fees & utility connections: several counties charge substantial impact fees per new dwelling unit — affects total project cost.
Installation licensing (manufactured only): must be set by a Florida-licensed installer to FAC 15C-1 standards (tie-downs, foundation, skirting).
Homestead/tax: adding an ADU is protected from reassessment of the homestead under 2025 state law (SB 184) — a selling point, verify current status.

chatbot_question_flow: 1. Get the property address (or at least city + county).
2. Determine jurisdiction: inside city limits -> city rules govern (flag if we lack a city record); unincorporated -> use the county record.
3. Which product: ADU on an existing home's lot, or a primary manufactured/modular home on its own land?
4. Look up the county record: adu_allowed / manufactured_allowed / modular_allowed + conditions.
5. Run universal_checks (HOA, flood, wind zone, septic, fees).
6. Give the answer with the verified/unverified caveat and the zoning dept contact from the record.

field_guide.adu_allowed: yes | no | conditional | unknown — is an accessory dwelling unit permitted?
field_guide.min_lot_size: Minimum lot size for an ADU as a descriptive string (e.g. '7,000 sq ft', '1 acre'), or null if none/unknown
field_guide.max_adu_size_sqft: Max ADU floor area as a descriptive string (rules are often formulas like '750 sq ft or 35% of primary, whichever is less'), or null if unknown
field_guide.manufactured_allowed: yes | no | conditional | unknown — manufactured homes on the parcel
field_guide.modular_allowed: yes | no | conditional | unknown — modular (state-approved) homes; usually allowed anywhere site-built is
field_guide.zoning_dept_url: Link to the jurisdiction's zoning/planning page
field_guide.verified: true | false — has a human confirmed this record?
field_guide.manufactured_districts: Zoning districts where a manufactured (HUD) home is allowed as a primary dwelling under CURRENT county code (pre-2027 law), plus placement standards (age limits, skirting, appearance). Null if not yet researched - then answer 'district-dependent, call the county'.

statewide_baseline.note: Florida encourages ADUs (F.S. 163.31771) but leaves adoption to local governments. Modular homes built to the Florida Building Code must be treated like site-built homes statewide. Manufactured homes must meet HUD code and are subject to local zoning district limits.

manufactured_home_law_2027.bill: HB 399 (2026), signed March 27, 2026; paired with SB 594 (repeals the 20% SHIP funding cap for manufactured housing)
manufactured_home_law_2027.rule: Starting Jan 1, 2027, local governments MUST allow modular and manufactured homes BY RIGHT in ANY zoning district where single-family detached homes are allowed - including multifamily, agricultural, commercial or industrial districts that permit single-family. Local governments may apply uniform architectural/design/compatibility standards but may NOT treat factory-built homes more restrictively than site-built homes. Conflicting local regulations are void and unenforceable.
manufactured_home_law_2027.chatbot_guidance: Before Jan 1, 2027: use the county's manufactured_districts field (or say 'district-dependent, call the county'). On/after Jan 1, 2027: the default answer becomes 'a new manufactured home is allowed wherever a site-built single-family home is allowed, subject to uniform appearance standards' - counties may lag in updating their codes, but the state law controls. Wind-zone unit rating still applies regardless (see manufactured_home_wind_zones).

counties.adu_conditions: One ADU allowed as an accessory use in A, RE, RE-1, R-1aa, R-1a, R-1b and R-1c districts, and it does NOT count against residential density. Generous size cap. Zoning permit ($70) needs a site plan, floor plan and ADU affidavit; then a building permit for construction/placement. Gainesville city has its own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 1,700 sq ft or 50% of the principal structure, whichever is LARGER
counties.setbacks: Follows the primary structure's district setbacks.
counties.permit_notes: Apply via Citizenserve portal; Building Division 352-374-5243.
counties.source: Alachua County Code Sec. 404.24; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Very ADU-friendly: allowed in most residential zones (LDC Sec. 30-5.35), max 850 sq ft, and uniquely permits BOTH one attached AND one detached ADU per lot (lots under 5,445 sq ft limited to one). City launched a FREE pre-approved ADU plans program in April 2025 - big cost saver to mention to customers.
municipalities.min_lot_size: None; lots under 5,445 sq ft (1/8 acre) limited to one ADU
municipalities.permit_notes: Free ADU plans: Building Division 352-334-5050 / building@gainesvillefl.gov.
municipalities.source: Gainesville LDC Sec. 30-5.35/30-5.36 + Apr 2025 free-plans initiative; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Macclenny. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.permit_notes: County seat: Starke. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: As of mid-2026 unincorporated Brevard does NOT allow full ADUs by right — only detached 'guesthouses' for family/temporary guests (no separate meters, cannot be rented). County must adopt a conforming ADU ordinance by Dec 1, 2026 under state law, so this is expected to change. City rules vary: Palm Bay allows ADUs in residential zones; Cocoa does not. Re-verify before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: Confirm current status with Brevard Planning & Development — rules being rewritten for 2026.
counties.source: Brevard County; verified Jul 2026 — ordinance in transition

municipalities.adu_conditions: The ADU-friendly spot in Brevard (county itself is still guesthouse-only): ADUs permitted by right in single-family zones under the city LDC (Sec. 174.003 / 185.118). Max 800 sq ft or 50% of the primary dwelling, whichever is less. Owner-occupancy of one unit generally required, confirmed by affidavit at permit stage. PUD properties need ordinance-level confirmation first.
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 800 sq ft or 50% of primary dwelling, whichever is less
municipalities.permit_notes: If the lot is in a PUD, confirm the PUD's ADU provisions with Growth Management before quoting.
municipalities.source: Palm Bay LDC Sec. 174.003/185.118; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Broward has NO single county-wide ADU standard — rules are set by each city (Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, etc.). Most require an annually-renewed Certificate of Use obtainable only by the owner, and many require owner-occupancy of one unit. County is in the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone — strictest construction standards. Customer MUST check their specific city's Development Services.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Varies by city; typically 400-800 sq ft and up to ~50% of the primary dwelling
counties.permit_notes: HVHZ-compliant construction required countywide.
counties.source: Broward is municipality-governed for ADUs; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Allowed under ULDC Sec. 47-19.2 but tightly: max 600 sq ft or 49% of the primary home (whichever is less), 1 bedroom + 1 bathroom (or studio), one ADU per lot. Owner must live on-site in either unit. No rentals under 30 days. A rented ADU needs an annually renewed Certificate of Use with yearly inspection. One extra off-street parking space required. HVHZ construction standards.
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 600 sq ft or 49% of primary home, whichever is less; 1 bed/1 bath max
municipalities.permit_notes: Annual Certificate of Use for rented ADUs - budget for the recurring inspection.
municipalities.source: Fort Lauderdale ULDC Sec. 47-19.2; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Blountstown. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: As of mid-2026 unincorporated Charlotte allows detached 'guest suites' but they may have living/sanitary facilities ONLY — no cooking facilities — so they are NOT full rentable ADUs yet. Detached accessory structures are size-limited: on lots under 0.5 acre, total of all detached accessory structures cannot exceed 1% of parcel size or 1,000 sq ft (whichever greater); on lots 0.5 acre+, up to 3,000 sq ft total. County must add a true by-right ADU under state law by Dec 1, 2026. Punta Gorda self-governs. Confirm current ADU status before quoting.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Guest suite governed by accessory-structure caps (1,000 sq ft on small lots; up to 3,000 sq ft total on 0.5 acre+). No kitchen currently allowed.
counties.setbacks: Per zoning district; detached accessory structures 400-1,500 sq ft limited to 12 ft wall/leg height.
counties.permit_notes: Special exception available to exceed accessory-structure size caps. Contact Community Development.
counties.source: Charlotte County LDC (accessory structures / detached guest suite); verified Jul 2026 — true rentable ADU not yet in code
counties.manufactured_districts: Dedicated districts: MHC (Manufactured Home Conventional, code 60), MHS (Mobile Home Subdivision, code 61), MHP (Manufactured Home Park, code 62). REMINDER: Charlotte is a WIND ZONE III county - only Zone III-rated units can be installed.

counties.adu_conditions: Guest cottages / garage apartments with living units (ADUs) allowed for noncommercial occupancy. Must be smaller in coverage/height/mass than the primary home, have its own entrance and full kitchen, and at least one off-street parking space. Building permit always required (reviewed under IRC 2021). Inverness and Crystal River set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 850 sq ft (county guest cottage); state law bars caps below 1,000 sq ft for compliant applications
counties.permit_notes: Permitting may require in-person submission of blueprints/survey — call ahead.
counties.source: Citrus County LDC (Ord. 2021); verified Jul 2026
counties.manufactured_districts: UNCONFIRMED. District-by-district lookup needed (LDC Ch. 2/3) - call Growth Management 352-527-5239.

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed via conditional-use permit in PUD, AG, AR, AR-1, AR-2, RA, RB and RE districts. Owner must occupy the (homesteaded) primary residence; ADU cannot be sold separately or the lot subdivided to split it off. One ADU per single-family dwelling; may be detached or a garage apartment, and must be subordinate to and match the primary home. One extra off-street parking space required.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 375 sq ft min; max 40% of primary building's gross floor area or 1,000 sq ft, whichever is less
counties.setbacks: Per residential district; ADU subordinate in height/coverage.
counties.permit_notes: Requires a Conditional Use Permit through Planning & Zoning.
counties.source: Clay County ADU Requirements (county site); verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: A rentable 'guest unit' (ADU) is allowed by right in RE and R1-E districts, and by conditional-use approval in R1-15A, R1-15, R1-10, R1-10A. Separately, non-rentable GUESTHOUSES are allowed on lots of 1+ acre (43,560 sq ft) but may NOT be rented — renting one violates the LDC. Golden Gate Estates is a common ADU/guest-home area. Naples, Marco Island have their own codes.
counties.min_lot_size: 1 acre (43,560 sq ft) for a guesthouse
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Guest unit ~750 sq ft or 35% of primary (whichever less); guesthouse up to 40% of principal dwelling
counties.setbacks: Guesthouse min 20 ft from principal structure.
counties.permit_notes: Distinguish rentable guest unit vs. non-rentable guesthouse. Contact Growth Management.
counties.source: Collier County LDC Sec. 5.03.03; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Lake City. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Jacksonville (consolidated with Duval) allows ADUs in most low-density single-family zones (Ord. 2022-0448-E). Owner must live in the primary home OR the ADU — not rent out both. ADU cannot be taller than the primary home. NOT permitted under city rules in the Beaches (Atlantic Beach, Neptune Beach, Jacksonville Beach) or Baldwin, which self-govern.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 750 sq ft or 25% of the primary residence, whichever is less
counties.setbacks: Per single-family district. 1 extra parking space required for ADUs 500+ sq ft.
counties.permit_notes: Full kitchen allowed since 2022. Submit zoning inquiry to Planning & Development.
counties.source: City of Jacksonville Zoning Code Sec. 656.403; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Escambia's Land Development Code (Sec. 4-7.3) DOES address ADUs accessory to single-family homes — they are permitted subject to size limits set by the applicable zoning district (exact per-district square-footage caps not published in summaries; confirm the parcel's district). Special placement: on lots 10+ acres an accessory structure/ADU may sit in the front yard if 60+ ft from the front line; waterfront lots need conditional-use (board of adjustment) approval. County must ensure a by-right ADU under state law by Dec 1, 2026. City of Pensacola has its own framework.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Limited by the zoning district's size rules (varies by district) — confirm exact cap with the county
counties.setbacks: Per LDC Sec. 4-7.3 and the underlying district.
counties.permit_notes: Confirm the parcel's zoning district size cap. Escambia Development Services (unincorporated) or City of Pensacola Planning.
counties.source: Escambia County LDC Sec. 4-7.3; verified Jul 2026 — ADUs allowed subject to district size limits (exact caps to confirm)

counties.adu_conditions: County code doesn't yet clearly name 'ADUs' — it allows detached 'guest or servant quarters' for guests/inhabitants of the principal home, NOT for renting or selling. County must allow one ADU by right under state law by Dec 1, 2026. Palm Coast, Bunnell, Flagler Beach set their own rules. Verify directly.
counties.permit_notes: Contact Flagler County Planning & Zoning; ADU rules being established for 2026.
counties.source: Flagler County Code (guest/servant quarters provision); verified Jul 2026 — no rentable ADU in code yet, ordinance due 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Restrictive as of mid-2026: ADU allowed only as an ATTACHED addition to an owner-occupied single-family home — detached units not permitted under current code. Property must be owner-occupied. County must conform to state law (allow one ADU by right) by Dec 1, 2026, so this may loosen. Brooksville and Weeki Wachee city rules differ.
counties.permit_notes: Detached ADU (typical for a delivered home) likely NOT allowed yet — confirm with Hernando Zoning before quoting.
counties.source: Hernando County zoning; verified Jul 2026 (attached-only restriction)
counties.manufactured_districts: UNCONFIRMED. District list not published in summaries - identify the parcel's district via the Property Appraiser GIS map, then check Appendix A of the county code, or call 352-754-4050.

counties.adu_conditions: County treats these as 'accessory structures' / guest houses, NOT true ADUs: a guest house/cottage may NOT include cooking facilities and may NOT be rented. No defined ADU size cap in current code. County must allow one rentable ADU by right under state law by Dec 1, 2026. Sebring, Avon Park, Lake Placid set their own rules. Confirm current status before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: Guest house = no kitchen, no renting. Contact Highlands Planning & Zoning (863-402-6650); rules updating for 2026.
counties.source: Highlands County LDR Ch. 12 (guest house provision); verified Jul 2026 — no rentable ADU in code yet
counties.manufactured_districts: Allowed in R-1A (single-family residential) and M-1 (mobile/manufactured home subdivision, medium density). Full-time RV living not permitted in R-1A (only temporarily while building). Unusually manufactured-friendly: R-1A residential lots qualify.

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in single-family residential zones (incl. R-1) in unincorporated county. Owner must occupy either the primary home or the ADU. One ADU per lot. Applies to unincorporated county; Tampa, Temple Terrace, Plant City set their own rules.
counties.min_lot_size: 7,000 sq ft (min); 14,520 sq ft required to exceed 750 sq ft via variance
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 750 sq ft or 35% of primary home's living area, whichever is less (up to 1,200 sq ft or 25% by variance on lots >=14,520 sq ft)
counties.setbacks: Follows the single-family district; combined lot coverage cap ~35-40%.
counties.permit_notes: County building permit; confirm parcel zoning with Development Services before ordering.
counties.source: Hillsborough County Land Development Code Sec. 6.11.02 (Municode); verified via county code Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Full rentable ADUs allowed only in designated neighborhoods/overlays (Seminole Heights, East Tampa, Tampa Heights, Lowry Park Central, Sulphur Springs, Temple Crest, Wellswood, Riverside Heights, and others - about 10 areas), max 950 sq ft. Everywhere else citywide only an 'Extended Family Residence' (EFR) is allowed - capped at 600 sq ft and restricted to family members. Owner must occupy either unit (Oct 2024 reform). Detached ADU cannot sit in front of the principal home; 50% impervious-surface cap; 1 off-street space; separate water/electric/gas meters required.
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 950 sq ft in ADU-eligible zones; 600 sq ft EFR elsewhere
municipalities.setbacks: Per district; ADU behind or beside the principal structure.
municipalities.permit_notes: Check whether the address falls in an ADU-eligible neighborhood first - that is the deciding question in Tampa.
municipalities.source: City of Tampa ADU FAQ (Feb 2024) + Oct 2024 reforms; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Bonifay. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: ADUs allowed on residentially zoned lots. One ADU per lot on parcels 200,000 sq ft or less. Must match the appearance of the principal structure (except garage conversions). Vero Beach and Sebastian set their own rules (Sebastian adopted its own ADU ordinance in 2025).
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: On lots 1 acre or less: 50% of the principal structure's heated/enclosed area or 1,200 sq ft, whichever is less
counties.permit_notes: Contact Indian River County Planning & Zoning.
counties.source: Indian River County Code Ch. 911; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Marianna. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Monticello. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Mayo. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: One accessory apartment OR ADU per single-family residence. Must match/harmonize with the principal structure. IMPORTANT for delivered homes: must sit on a solid foundation with permanent skirting and any wheels/transport mechanisms removed (a manufactured home is fine if set this way). Cities (Clermont, Leesburg, Mount Dora, Tavares) set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 1,300 sq ft or 70% of the principal structure's living area, whichever is GREATER
counties.permit_notes: Contact Lake County Planning & Zoning.
counties.source: Lake County Land Development Regs Sec. 3.07.00; verified Jul 2026
counties.manufactured_districts: Allowed in Agricultural or Residential districts on parcels of at least 5 ACRES (smaller lots generally cannot take a manufactured home under current code). Not in the 100-year flood zone unless LDR flood requirements are met. 5-acre minimum is the binding constraint until the 2027 law.

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in single-family residential zones and Agricultural (AG-2) districts. One accessory apartment OR one ADU per single-family residence. Must be on the same lot as the primary home and match its exterior design (except garage conversions). Cape Coral, Fort Myers, Bonita Springs set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 750 sq ft or 35% of the primary residence's living area, whichever is less
counties.permit_notes: Consult Lee County Land Development Code for setbacks specific to your district.
counties.source: Lee County Land Development Code; verified Jul 2026 (owner-occupancy not confirmed)

municipalities.adu_conditions: Currently the MOST restrictive big city in SW Florida: no full rentable ADUs. Only 'guest houses'/guest quarters in the Residential Estate (RE) district, on lots of 40,000+ sq ft, one story, max 30% of the primary structure, no kitchen-equipped rental unit. Typical 10,000 sq ft Cape Coral lots do NOT qualify. Expected to change by Dec 1, 2026 under state mandate - re-check status before turning a customer away.
municipalities.permit_notes: Standard residential lots don't qualify today; watch for the 2026 ordinance update.
municipalities.source: Cape Coral LDC (guest houses, RE district); verified Jul 2026

counties.manufactured_districts: Dedicated districts: MH-1, MH-4, MHC-1, MHC-2 and Mobile-Home Planned Developments; AG-2 permits related residential uses. Full district list needs county confirmation. WIND ZONE III county - only Zone III-rated units.

counties.adu_conditions: City of Tallahassee simplified its rules in March 2024 to allow ADUs in ALL residential zones citywide, 2-story allowed. Long-term (30+ day) rentals OK; short-term needs a separate license and is often restricted. Unincorporated Leon County rules may differ — verify jurisdiction.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 500 sq ft min, 1,200 sq ft max (Tallahassee)
counties.permit_notes: Building permit via City of Tallahassee Building Inspection (~5-14 workdays).
counties.source: City of Tallahassee 2024 ADU ordinance; verified Jul 2026 (unincorporated county not confirmed)

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Bronson. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Bristol. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Madison. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Recently loosened rules. Allowed in A, A-1, RSF, PD and Village districts. One subordinate structure (ADU OR guest house) on lots under 5 acres; more with acreage. Both detached ADUs and garage apartments allowed. PROHIBITED in the Whitfield Residential Overlay and Bayshore Gardens Park & Recreation districts. Bradenton and other cities set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Up to 80% of the primary residence (former 750 sq ft cap eliminated)
counties.setbacks: Detached ADU min 10 ft from rear line if windows face neighbors.
counties.permit_notes: Contact Manatee County Building & Development Services.
counties.source: Manatee County revised ADU regulations; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed on most parcels with enough land; detached ADUs generally need ~1 acre. No explicit square-footage cap as long as the ADU stays subordinate to the primary home. Septic capacity must accommodate the extra unit. City of Ocala has its own rules; The Villages HOAs largely prohibit detached ADUs.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: No fixed cap — must remain subordinate to primary dwelling
counties.permit_notes: Confirm with Marion County Planning/Zoning; septic review likely.
counties.source: Marion County Land Development Code; verified Jul 2026 (specifics vary by parcel)
counties.manufactured_districts: Allowed BY RIGHT in A-1, A-2, A-3, RR-1 and R-4 (LDC Sec. 4.3.5/4.3.6). Placement standards: approved foundation compatible with nearby site-built homes, stucco/block/brick-look skirting on all sides, garage or carport required where nearby homes have them, paved driveway, minimum 20 ft main body width, orientation matching neighboring site-built homes. One of the clearest codes in the state - strong manufactured-home market.

counties.adu_conditions: ADUs permitted subject to development standards. Must be on a concrete foundation/slab and located in the rear or side yard of the principal dwelling. Stuart, Jupiter Island, Sewall's Point and other municipalities set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 850 sq ft or 50% of the primary dwelling, whichever is less
counties.setbacks: Rear or side yard placement required.
counties.permit_notes: Contact Martin County Growth Management.
counties.source: Martin County LDR; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in AU, EU, RU and GU zones within the Urban Development Boundary via a Certificate of Use (renewed annually). Owner must live in the primary home or the ADU as principal residence; only the owner may obtain the CU. One ADU per lot; may be rented (min 30 days). County is in the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone — strictest construction standards. 30+ municipalities set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 400-800 sq ft (RU-1 districts)
counties.setbacks: Per zoning district; 1 extra parking space, max 2 vehicles.
counties.permit_notes: Conditional Use / Certificate of Use required + HVHZ-compliant build.
counties.source: Miami-Dade County code / county ADU page; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: City of Miami (Miami 21 zoning code): ADUs allowed in T3-L transect zones; an expansion to T3-R/T3-O passed first reading Feb 2025 - confirm current adoption status with City Planning. High-Velocity Hurricane Zone construction standards apply to everything built here.
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: Per Miami 21 transect standards - confirm with city
municipalities.permit_notes: Ask City of Miami Planning whether the parcel's transect (T3-L/T3-R/T3-O) currently allows an ADU.
municipalities.source: Miami 21 code + Feb 2025 expansion reading; verified Jul 2026 (adoption status to confirm)

municipalities.adu_conditions: Hialeah operates an 'Extended Family Living Quarters' program rather than a standard rentable-ADU ordinance - historically for family occupancy. City must conform to state ADU law by Dec 1, 2026. Confirm current status and whether rental ADUs are now permitted.
municipalities.permit_notes: Ask Hialeah Planning & Zoning for the Extended Family Living Quarters form / current ADU rules.
municipalities.source: City of Hialeah Extended Family Living Quarters program; verified Jul 2026 (rentable ADU status UNCONFIRMED)

counties.adu_conditions: SPECIAL CASE — the Florida Keys. New dwelling units (incl. ADUs) require a permit allocation under the competitive ROGO/NROGO Rate-of-Growth system, which is severely capped, plus a tier-based environmental score. An ADU needs a min 3,000 sq ft lot and is capped at 800 sq ft / 40% of the principal structure, max 2 bedrooms. NOTE: affordable/replacement units must meet current Florida Building Code and CANNOT be a mobile home. Also HVHZ + coastal flood construction. Islamorada, Key West, Marathon, Layton self-govern. Expect long waits and heavy restrictions.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 800 sq ft or 40% of the principal structure, whichever is less; max 2 bedrooms
counties.permit_notes: Must win a ROGO allocation — this is the binding constraint in the Keys, not zoning.
counties.source: Monroe County LDC Ch. 138 (ROGO) + Sec. 22.16.050; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed BY RIGHT in all residential districts that permit single-family homes. One per parcel. Max height 25 ft; detached units min 6 ft setback. If the ADU is rented, the main home must be the owner's primary residence with an active homestead exemption. Fernandina Beach (Amelia Island) has its own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 50% of the home's square footage (excl. garage), never more than 800 sq ft footprint
counties.setbacks: Detached ADU min 6 ft; max height 25 ft.
counties.permit_notes: Building permit via Nassau County Building Dept.
counties.source: Nassau County LDC Sec. 28.15; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: County code doesn't use 'ADU' but permits guest houses and garage apartments in ALL zoning districts (LDC Sec. 2.21.05). One guest house per lot; must be detached from the principal dwelling (may connect via a covered walkway). Size limit: the guest house cannot exceed the total square footage of the principal structure. County must add a conforming by-right ADU under state law by Dec 1, 2026. Fort Walton Beach, Crestview, Destin, Niceville self-govern.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Cannot exceed the square footage of the principal (primary) structure
counties.permit_notes: One detached guest house/garage apartment per lot. Contact Okaloosa County Growth Management.
counties.source: Okaloosa County LDC Sec. 2.21.05 (guest houses / garage apartments); verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: County permits 'accessory apartments' in residentially zoned areas (similar to ADUs). Built to Florida Building Code, currently capped small (~400 sq ft, min 6'8" ceiling), requiring a building permit, inspections and Certificate of Occupancy. Must allow ADUs under state law (HB 943 / SB 1730, 2025) — confirm current size cap. City of Okeechobee self-governs.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: ~400 sq ft under current county code (state law may override upward)
counties.permit_notes: Contact Okeechobee County Community Development.
counties.source: Okeechobee County code; verified Jul 2026 (size cap likely changing under state law)

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed as a special exception in any residential or agricultural district. Single ownership required; owner must occupy the primary or the ADU, and the primary must be homesteaded. One ADU per lot, max 2 bedrooms. No separate water/power meter for attached units. Must be compatible with the primary home and neighborhood. City of Orlando has its own ADU code.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Min 500 sq ft; max 45% of primary living area or 1,000 sq ft, whichever is less
counties.permit_notes: Special-exception approval + building permit. Contact Zoning@ocfl.net.
counties.source: Orange County Code Sec. 38-1426; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Allowed under City Code Ch. 58 Part 3A. Max 1,000 sq ft (only 500 sq ft on a non-conforming lot of record; up to 700 sq ft in historic districts with Certificate of Appropriateness). ADU must be smaller than the principal home, and combined floor-area ratio cannot exceed 0.50 (or the district max, whichever is less). Notably more permissive than unincorporated Orange County (no special exception needed).
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 1,000 sq ft (500 on non-conforming lots; 700 in historic districts)
municipalities.permit_notes: City Planning handles ADU review; standard building permit.
municipalities.source: Orlando City Code Ch. 58 Part 3A; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Osceola's Land Development Code permits 'Accessory Living Units' (its term for ADUs) in residential zones (Ch. 3, Art. 3.6 Residential Use Siting Standards). A qualifying accessory living unit does NOT count toward the parcel's density, and such accessory structures are exempt from certain setbacks. Exact square-footage cap not published in summaries — confirm the parcel's district. Under state law each qualifying parcel may have up to two ADUs (min 150 sq ft each), smaller than the primary home. Kissimmee and St. Cloud self-govern.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Not published in summaries — confirm district cap with the county (state floor: allowed up to 1,000 sq ft, not restricted below 500)
counties.setbacks: Accessory living units exempt from certain setbacks (LDC Ch. 3).
counties.permit_notes: Ask for 'Accessory Living Unit' standards, LDC Ch. 3 Art. 3.6. Osceola Community Development.
counties.source: Osceola County LDC Ch. 3 Art. 3.6 (Accessory Living Units); verified Jul 2026 — allowed; exact size cap to confirm

municipalities.adu_conditions: Allowed under LDC Sec. 14-6-2: min 500 sq ft, max 800 sq ft or 40% of the primary home's gross floor area, whichever is less (so a 1,400 sq ft house caps at 560 sq ft - primary must be 2,000+ sq ft to reach the full 800). A DETACHED ADU needs a lot at least 1.5x the district minimum (e.g. RA-2: 9,000 min -> 13,500 sq ft for detached). No short-term rentals in standard residential ADU zones.
municipalities.min_lot_size: District minimum; 1.5x district minimum for a detached ADU
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 800 sq ft or 40% of primary GFA, whichever is less (min 500)
municipalities.permit_notes: Call Planning & Zoning 407-518-2140 with the parcel ID to confirm the district in minutes.

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in most residential zones on the same lot as a single-family home. Independent unit with kitchen, 1 bedroom, 1 bath. Must meet the primary dwelling's parking/dimensional requirements. Jupiter, Wellington, Boca Raton and other cities set their own rules — verify county vs. city jurisdiction.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 800 sq ft on lots under 1 acre; 1,000 sq ft on lots of 1+ acre
counties.permit_notes: Pre-application consultation offered by the county building dept.
counties.source: Palm Beach County ULDC; verified Jul 2026 (owner-occupancy not confirmed)

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed as accessory to single-family development in districts that permit single-family dwellings and in single-family portions of MPUDs. Must be smaller than the main house, on a permanent foundation, meeting FBC. Rentals must be 180+ days unless special approval. On-site parking required. Septic/well parcels need FL Dept. of Health approval.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 1,200 sq ft (larger needs special exception + public hearing)
counties.setbacks: Same height, setback and lot-coverage rules as the principal dwelling.
counties.permit_notes: See mypas.co/adu; homeowner affidavit required.
counties.source: Pasco County LDC Sec. 402; verified Jul 2026
counties.manufactured_districts: Dedicated districts: R-1MH (Single-Family/Mobile Home) and R-MH (Mobile Home District, LDC Sec. 511); also placed in AR-type rural districts. KEY LIMITS: home must be NO MORE THAN 5 YEARS OLD when placed, wheels/transport gear removed, residential-look skirting, and lap/T1-11-style siding comparable to site-built homes. The 5-year age limit is a hard gate - ask the home's model year first.

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in all unincorporated residential districts (R-A, R-E, R-R, R-1, R-2, R-3, RMH, R-4, R-5, RM, RPD). Owner must occupy the primary home or the ADU (verified via homestead exemption). One ADU per parcel; must match the primary home's exterior finish and roof style. Development review fees waived since Jul 2024. Cities (St. Pete, Clearwater, Largo) have separate rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 1,000 sq ft standard; 750 sq ft in Coastal Storm Areas / FEMA flood zones
counties.setbacks: Must meet the district's setback and lot-coverage rules.
counties.permit_notes: Contact Pinellas Planning & Zoning; building permit required.
counties.source: Pinellas County Code Ch. 138; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Allowed in NT, NTM-1 and NS districts. Min 375 sq ft; max 800 sq ft or 67% of the primary home, whichever is smaller. NT districts need a lot of at least 4,500 sq ft; NS districts need 10,000 sq ft OR an alley or corner lot. City provides an online ADU Map Lookup Tool - enter the address to check eligibility instantly.
municipalities.min_lot_size: 4,500 sq ft (NT); 10,000 sq ft or alley/corner lot (NS)
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 800 sq ft or 67% of primary home, whichever is smaller (min 375)
municipalities.setbacks: NS detached: 10 ft side (7.5 ft on alley lots).
municipalities.permit_notes: Use the city's ADU Map Lookup Tool or email DevRev@stpete.org with the address.
municipalities.source: St. Petersburg Code Sec. 16.50.010; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Clearwater has its own ADU framework (separate from Pinellas County): secondary unit with its own kitchen/bath/sleeping area on a single-family lot. Rentals require 30-31 day minimums (no Airbnb-style use). Exact size caps not published in summaries - state law bars caps below 1,000 sq ft for compliant applications. Confirm specifics with city Planning & Development.
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: Confirm with city (state floor: cannot be capped below 1,000 sq ft for compliant applications)
municipalities.source: City of Clearwater ADU page; verified Jul 2026 (size caps to confirm)

counties.adu_conditions: One detached ADU per residential lot (Ord. 25-0415). Must meet Florida Building Code on a permanent foundation. IMPORTANT: mobile/manufactured homes are explicitly PROHIBITED as ADUs — modular homes built to FBC are fully allowed. (Note: 2025 state law now defines manufactured homes built on/after Jan 1 2025 to HUD standards as ADUs, which may preempt this local ban — worth asking the county.) Short-term/vacation rentals banned; long-term (30+ day) rentals OK. Cities (Lakeland, Winter Haven) set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 50% of the primary residence, max 1,000 sq ft
counties.permit_notes: Contact Polk County Growth Management.
counties.source: Polk County Ord. 25-0415; verified Jul 2026

municipalities.adu_conditions: Allowed in urban areas of the city: min 300 sq ft, max 800 sq ft or 40% of the principal dwelling, whichever is less. Requires Planning & Zoning Board approval (plus Historic Preservation Board in historic districts). City rules apply instead of Polk County's - note the county's manufactured-home-as-ADU ban is a COUNTY rule; ask the city what it allows.
municipalities.max_adu_size_sqft: 800 sq ft or 40% of principal dwelling, whichever is less (min 300)
municipalities.setbacks: 5 ft interior side and rear; front min 5 ft behind the principal facade; side-street same as principal.
municipalities.permit_notes: P&Z Board approval required - budget extra review time.
municipalities.source: Lakeland zoning (urban-area ADU standards); verified Jul 2026

counties.manufactured_districts: Set-up permit required before placing/relocating any manufactured home (county Ch. on manufactured homes). Commonly placed in A/RR (Agricultural/Residential-Rural) and RC districts, but the exact by-right district list needs confirmation from Table 2.1 of the LDC - call Growth Management 863-534-5985. Large manufactured-home market; county bans MH as ADUs but allows as primary dwellings in rural districts.

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in AE, AG, RE, R-1, R-2 and some commercial districts WITH a special-use permit. Guest houses/ADUs intended for temporary guests. One extra bedroom may be approved by the zoning board. Palatka files all applications through the county first.
counties.min_lot_size: 1 acre (unless served by central water & sewer)
counties.permit_notes: Special-use permit required. Planning & Development 386-329-0491; zoning 386-329-0316.
counties.source: Putnam County Code; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Accessory family units / ADUs allowed in single-family residential zones but generally cannot be rented commercially. Must match the primary home architecturally; parking rules vary by neighborhood. IMPORTANT for this business: a manufactured/mobile home used as an accessory family unit requires a minimum 2-acre lot properly zoned for manufactured/mobile homes. 'Guest houses' may have only limited kitchen facilities, can't be occupied by the same tenant over 30 days/year, and cannot be rented. St. Augustine city is separate.
counties.min_lot_size: 2 acres if the accessory unit is a manufactured/mobile home
counties.permit_notes: Confirm zoning district allows manufactured homes before quoting one as an ADU.
counties.source: St. Johns County Land Development Code; verified Jul 2026
counties.manufactured_districts: Manufactured/mobile homes only in districts zoned for them; as an accessory family unit requires a 2+ acre lot (already noted in adu_conditions).

counties.adu_conditions: Confirmed: as of mid-2026 the UNINCORPORATED county's land development code does not yet reference ADUs (only ongoing discussion of 'accessible dwelling units'/tiny homes) — a conforming by-right ADU ordinance is required by Dec 1, 2026. City status varies: Fort Pierce permits ADUs (and under state law cannot require owner-occupancy or cap size below 1,000 sq ft), but Port St. Lucie's zoning code does NOT yet list ADUs as a permitted accessory use — see its municipality record. Check the address's jurisdiction first.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: State law bars caps below 1,000 sq ft for compliant applications (city ADUs)
counties.permit_notes: If inside Port St. Lucie or Fort Pierce, use the city's ADU rules. Unincorporated county rules pending 2026.
counties.source: St. Lucie County LDC + city rules; verified Jul 2026 — unincorporated county has no ADU ordinance yet, cities do

municipalities.adu_conditions: CORRECTION vs earlier assumptions: Port St. Lucie's single-family zoning code (Ch. 158) does NOT currently list ADUs as a permitted accessory use - a separate second living unit is not allowed by right today. A conforming ADU ordinance is required by the state mandate; expect standards to be adopted. City enforces the 8th Edition (2023) Florida Building Code. Pre-application consultation with the city recommended.
municipalities.permit_notes: Not allowed by right yet - check for the new ordinance before quoting. Pre-app consultations available.
municipalities.source: Port St. Lucie Zoning Code Ch. 158 (Secs. 158.073/158.217); verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: ADUs allowed as 'guest cottages'. Must sit 60 ft from the front property line and meet the principal structure's side/rear setbacks. NOTE: park-model tiny homes are NOT permitted as guest cottages. Milton, Gulf Breeze, Jay set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Up to 50% of the main dwelling's total floor area
counties.setbacks: 60 ft from front line; side/rear per principal structure.
counties.permit_notes: Contact Santa Rosa County Development Services.
counties.source: Santa Rosa County Land Development Code; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed in RSF, RSF/PUD and RE districts in unincorporated county. Owner-occupancy required; short-term (under 30 day) rentals banned. NOTE: City of Sarasota is very different — no owner-occupancy and 7-day-min STRs allowed. Verify county vs. city jurisdiction.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 500 sq ft (unincorporated county)
counties.permit_notes: Contact Sarasota County Planning & Development Services.
counties.source: Sarasota County Code Sec. 5.4 / Appendix A; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: One ADU per single-family detached home, administratively approved in A-1, RC-1, single-family districts and single-family PDs. Lot must be at least 50 ft wide and 5,000 sq ft. One off-street parking space required (paved/stabilized, same driveway). Cities (Sanford, Altamonte Springs, Oviedo) set their own rules.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 35% of the primary structure's gross floor area or 1,000 sq ft, whichever is less
counties.permit_notes: Administrative approval by Planning & Development Division.
counties.source: Seminole County Land Development Code (Ord. 2021-13); verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Sumter's ADU is a 'Family Accessory Cottage' — one per lot of 0.5 acre or more, attached or freestanding, sharing the same access/driveway. KEY RESTRICTION: occupancy limited to family of the principal-structure resident (parent, child, grandparent, grandchild, or sibling of the owner/spouse) — it CANNOT be rented to the public or sold separately. The Villages CDD deed restrictions may add more limits inside that footprint. County must broaden to a rentable ADU under state law by Dec 1, 2026.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: 400 sq ft min; max 40% of the principal structure's gross floor area, not to exceed 1,200 sq ft
counties.permit_notes: Currently family-only; a customer wanting a rentable ADU should confirm the 2026 update status.
counties.source: Sumter County Family Accessory Cottage program; verified Jul 2026

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. Under Florida state law (SB 184 / SB 943, and SB 48 due Dec 1, 2026) the county must allow at least one ADU by right on single-family lots, so the practical answer is trending toward YES, but specifics (size cap, lot minimum, owner-occupancy) are NOT confirmed here. Modular homes (Florida Building Code) are allowed statewide; manufactured-home placement depends on the parcel zoning district. VERIFY directly before quoting.
counties.permit_notes: County seat: Live Oak. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Allowed by right on lots over 1 acre. One dedicated parking space for the ADU plus a shared common driveway required. Cities (Deltona, Daytona Beach, Port Orange, DeLand) set their own rules.
counties.min_lot_size: 1 acre for by-right; smaller lots allowed with size cap
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Lots 1+ acre: 50% of primary. Lots under 1 acre: 50% of primary living area or 1,200 sq ft. Garage apartments capped at 800 sq ft.
counties.permit_notes: See Volusia County ADU Regulations Handout & Checklist.
counties.source: Volusia County 2024 ADU Regulation Handout; verified Jul 2026
counties.manufactured_districts: Allowed in MH-1 through MH-8, FR (Forestry Resources), RC, RA, RR, and A-1 through A-4 (or a vested lot / lawful mobile home park). Zoning office: 386-736-5959 x12719, zoning@volusia.org (have the tax parcel number ready).

counties.adu_conditions: Rural county with no confirmed dedicated ADU ordinance as of mid-2026. Many such counties historically allow only non-rentable guest houses or family accessory units. counties.permit_notes: County seat: Crawfordville. Call the county Planning/Zoning or Building Dept to confirm current ADU rules.
counties.source: Rural baseline - Florida state ADU law; county-specific ADU ordinance UNCONFIRMED (Jul 2026)

counties.adu_conditions: Small ADUs under 800 sq ft allowed in ALL districts that permit residential development. Cannot be used as a short-term vacation rental separate from the primary home. Must be ancillary/compatible with the primary structure and neighborhood, meeting the same setbacks. ADU area counts toward impervious-surface/stormwater calcs. Some Neighborhood Plan Areas (e.g. Inlet Beach) add restrictions. DeFuniak Springs, Freeport, Paxton self-govern.
counties.max_adu_size_sqft: Under 800 sq ft
counties.permit_notes: See Walton County LDC Sec. 2.02.00(B)(3).
counties.source: Walton County LDC Sec. 2.02.00; verified Jul 2026
